interactions.summary: Jiang uses The Last Supper to show Dante's theology inside Da Vinci: even entertainment claims such as The Da Vinci Code point toward Jesus' humanity, while the painting's musical and biblical structure encodes divine love and forgiveness.

He reads the Mona Lisa as secretly alive: it changes as the viewer moves, proving that art exists fully only through engagement. He then turns to Raphael's papal-room frescoes as a Renaissance balance of philosophy, religion, poetry, and law.

Jiang interprets School of Athens as a staged debate between Plato's upward spiritual reality and Aristotle's earthly science, with color, gesture, depth, and surrounding figures organizing the two realities.

claims.claim: Jiang treats the Da Vinci Code reading of The Last Supper as entertaining rather than scholarly, but still useful for seeing the painting's emphasis on Jesus' humanity.
The Last Supper's hidden musical and biblical structures are read as encoding Lamentations' message that God always loves, forgives, and refuses hatred.
claims.temporal_scope: Interpretation of Da Vinci through Dante in this lecture.

claims.claim: The Mona Lisa shows that art becomes alive only when the viewer participates; its expression changes through engagement rather than static observation.
claims.temporal_scope: Interpretation of Mona Lisa in this lecture.

claims.claim: Raphael's papal apartment frescoes embody the Renaissance ideal of open-minded holistic learning by balancing philosophy, religion, poetry, and law.
claims.temporal_scope: Interpretation of Raphael in this lecture.

claims.claim: School of Athens dramatizes the split between Plato's heaven-oriented spiritual reality and Aristotle's earth-oriented science, while insisting both realities matter.

signature_moments.moment: Jesus in The Last Supper is calm because he has already forgiven Judas; the rage belongs to everyone else.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This anchors Jiang's Dante-through-Da-Vinci claim in a vivid moral image.

signature_moments.moment: The Mona Lisa is alive only when the viewer moves with it.
signature_moments.source_phrase: Only if you participate in the art does it become alive
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It states the lecture's participatory aesthetics in its most memorable form.

signature_moments.moment: Plato and Aristotle split the world as they walk: heaven upward, earth downward.
signature_moments.source_phrase: they're splitting the world into two
signature_moments.why_it_matters: The gesture compresses metaphysics, philosophy, and visual composition into one scene.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Raphael's fresco, officially Philosophy in Jiang's framing, used to stage Renaissance balance between philosophy, religion, poetry, law, and the Plato-Aristotle divide.
Da Vinci painting Jiang treats as evidence that art becomes alive through viewer participation.

uncertainty_notes.note: The transcript repeatedly renders Da Vinci as Dante in some art-analysis passages; context indicates Jiang is discussing Da Vinci's paintings while arguing Dante's influence.
